elements.section-2411-003: Quintessence transmutation from thawing to scaling freezing
elements.sent-2411-143: A remarkable consequence of the DM-DE interaction via the field-theoretic interaction term is the transmutation of the quintessence field from thawing to scaling freezing.
elements.sent-2411-144: A characteristic feature of thawing potentials is the departure of the DE equation of state from $-1$ at late times (close to $a=1$) while that of scaling freezing potentials is the convergence of the DE equation of state toward $-1$ close to $a=1$.
elements.sent-2411-145: In this analysis we have found out that the presence of a DM-DE interaction term of the form $\lambda\chi^2\phi^2/2$ can cause a transmutation from thawing to scaling freezing at some transition scale factor $a_t$.
elements.sent-2411-146: The secret lies in the behavior of the potential $V_2(\phi)+V_{12 (\phi,\chi)$ close to $a=1$ where $V_2+V_{12}\sim \phi^2$ which is exactly the behavior of the scaling freezing double exponential potential $V_S(\phi)=V(e^{-\lambda_1 \phi +e^{-\lambda_2 \phi )$ for $\lambda_1=-\lambda_2$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-147: We can see this clearly from the right panel of Fig.~\ref{fig3S where the orange curve represent the total potential $V_2(\phi)+V_{12 (\phi,\chi)$ and the blacked dashed curve is the fitting function which has a double exponential form where we found the fit parameters to be $\lambda_1=-\lambda_2=-18.2\,\,\text{m _{\rm pl ^{-1}$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-148: We have also examined the effect of the interaction strength $\lambda$ on the transition scale factor $a_t$ by running a Monte Carlo analysis of the model.
elements.sent-2411-149: In Figs.~\ref{fig4S and~\ref{fig5S , we show the correlation plots between the different parameters of the model.}}
elements.sent-2411-150: From Fig.~\ref{fig4S , it is clear that $a_t$ and $\lambda$ are strongly anti-correlated which means that a stronger interaction results in an earlier transition between the two quintessence states.}
elements.sent-2411-151: As discussed in the Letter, such a transition can be searched for in the data and its detection would provide a direct evidence of a dark energy and dark matter interaction.
elements.sent-2411-152: One of the important issues both in particle physics and cosmology relates to whether dark energy is a cosmological constant $\Lambda$, or is dynamical in nature such as quintessence.
elements.sent-2411-153: In this work, we discuss a model of quintessence interacting with dark matter and analyze the resulting phenomenology of the dark energy equation of state.
elements.sent-2411-154: We identify two regions where the equation of state behaves differently depending on the size of the dark matter-dark energy interaction strength.
elements.sent-2411-155: We show that the strong coupling region induces a transmutation of quintessence from thawing to freezing.
elements.sent-2411-156: Using the recent data release from the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI), we rule out this possibility of transmutation and investigate the weak coupling region to derive upper limits on the interaction strength.
elements.sent-2411-157: The interacting dark energy model is able to explain DESI's results without the equation of state crossing the phantom divide.
elements.sent-2411-158: Currently the concordance $\Lambda$CDM model is considered the Standard Model of cosmology and is successful at explaining a large amount of cosmological data.
elements.sent-2411-159: We use the Friedmann-Lema\^itre-Robertson-Walker metric in conformal time $\tau$ so that the line element is given by $\text{d s^2=a^2(\tau)\left[-\text{d}\tau^2+g_{ij}\text{d x^i \text{d x^j\right]$, where $a(\tau)$ is the scale factor.}}}
elements.sent-2411-160: In spite of the great success of Eq.~(\ref{eq1 ), some anomalies have recently emerged, with two of the prominent ones being the Hubble tension, $H_0$, and the degree of matter clustering, $S_8$~\cite{Abdalla:2022yfr,DiValentino:2025sru .}}
elements.sent-2411-161: Several suggestions to reduce the Hubble tension have been proposed which, to name a few, include interacting dark matter models~\cite{Pourtsidou:2013nha,Pourtsidou:2016ico,Linton:2017ged,Chamings:2019kcl,Pan:2019gop,Bonici:2018qli,Yang:2019uzo,Pan:2020zza,Wang:2016lxa,Wang:2024vmw,Boehmer:2015kta,Boehmer:2015sha,Potter:2011nv,DiValentino:2019jae,DiValentino:2019ffd,Escamilla:2023shf,Bernui:2023byc,Yang:2022csz,Amendola:1999er,Kase:2019veo,Perez:2021cvg,Garcia-Arroyo:2024tqq,Beyer:2010mt,Beyer:2014uqa,vandeBruck:2022xbk,Yang:2018euj,Lee:2022cyh , early dark energy~\cite{Doran:2006kp,Agrawal:2019lmo,Vagnozzi:2023nrq , decaying dark matter~\cite{Berezhiani:2015yta,Ibarra:2013cra as well as models introducing extra relativistic degrees of freedom~\cite{Aboubrahim:2022gjb,Fernandez-Martinez:2021ypo,Escudero:2021rfi,Baumann:2015rya,Brust:2017nmv,Blinov:2020hmc,Jacques:2013xr,Cuesta:2021kca,Vagnozzi:2019ezj .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-162: A current trend is to introduce an interaction term (or a source term) between dark matter (DM) and dark energy (DE) at the level of the continuity equations~\cite{Abdalla:2022yfr,Valiviita:2008iv,Gavela:2009cy,DiValentino:2019jae , so that \begin{equation}\label{eq2} \rho^\prime_\chi+3H(1+w_\chi)\rho_\chi=Q, \rho^\prime_\phi+3H(1+w_\phi)\rho_\phi=-Q. \label{eq3}\end{equation} Here $\rho_\chi$ and $\rho_\phi$ are the energy densities for DM and DE, $w_\chi$ and $w_\phi$ are the corresponding equations of state (EoS), with $w$ defined as the ratio of pressure $(p)$ to energy density ($\rho$) for a given species, $w=p/\rho$, and $^\prime\equiv \text{d /\text{d}\tau$.}}
elements.sent-2411-163: Other than the $H_0$ and $S_8$ tensions, other anomalies have emerged as well, specifically one related to the dark energy EoS.
elements.sent-2411-164: Thus, the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI)~\cite{DESI:2024mwx,DESI:2025zgx recent results have pointed toward a time-varying EoS.}
elements.sent-2411-165: In $\Lambda$CDM, the cosmological constant's EoS is $w_\Lambda=-1$ and so a time-varying $w$ may indicate some underlying new physics deviating from $\Lambda$CDM.
elements.sent-2411-166: Using the Chevallier-Polarski-Linder (CPL) parameterization~\cite{Chevallier:2000qy,Linder:2002et of the EoS, $w(a)=w_0+(1-a)w_a$, DESI DR1 reports a preference for $w_0>-1$ and $w_a<0$ with the $\mathrm{DESI~BAO +\mathrm{CMB +\mathrm{DESY 5$ data set at the $3.9\sigma$ level which grew to $4.2\sigma$ with DR2.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-167: Quintessence is a well known alternative to the cosmological constant which can provide a scenario for a dynamical EoS.
elements.sent-2411-168: Many models have been proposed in this direction leading to several classifications of quintessence models.

elements.sent-2411-170: Compared to CPL, these models produce more complicated parameterizations of the EoS which do not lead to the crossing of the phantom divide, something the CPL suffers from.
elements.sent-2411-171: In this work, we examine the impact of a field-theoretic model on cosmology, and in particular, we focus on the dark energy equation of state.
elements.sent-2411-172: Motivated by a previous work~\cite{Aboubrahim:2024spa,Aboubrahim:2025qat , where the authors have shown that such a field-theoretic approach can alleviate the $H_0$ tension and resolve the $S_8$ tension, we set out to explore the possibility of a dynamical EoS.}
elements.sent-2411-173: Furthermore, we drop Eqs.~(\ref{eq2 ) and~(\ref{eq3 ) which are inconsistent within a field theoretic framework and consider a field theoretic formulation where energy conservation is automatic.}}
elements.sent-2411-174: We show that in this framework DM-DE interaction can lead to a rich EoS phenomenology, where for a strong coupling, a transmutation of quintessence from thawing to scaling freezing occurs while a weaker coupling retains the thawing nature of quintessence.
elements.sent-2411-175: We investigate fits to the EoS in the two coupling regimes and examine the impact of the DM-DE coupling in bringing the model to agreement with DESI DR2.
elements.sent-2411-176: We show that while the DM-DE interaction can be an impactful parameter in aligning model predictions with DESI's results, a strong preference exists for non-interacting quintessence to explain DESI's DR2 results.
elements.sent-2411-177: In the analysis here we propose an alternative to $\Lambda$CDM of Eq.~(\ref{eq1 ) which is a model of quintessence interacting with dark matter (QCDM) based on a Lagrangian.}
elements.sent-2411-178: The action of this theory is given by \begin{equation}S_{\rm QCDM}=\int d^4 x\sqrt{-g} \left[\frac{1}{16\pi G}R+ {\cal L}_{\rm QCDM}\right],\end{equation} and the QCDM Lagrangian is \begin{equation}{\cal L}_{\rm QCDM}= \sum_i\frac{1}{2}\phi_i^{,\mu}\phi_{i,\mu}- V(\{\phi_i\},\{\phi_j\}),\end{equation} where \begin{equation}V=\sum_i^n V_i(\phi_i)+ V_{\rm int}(\{\phi_i\},\{\phi_j\}), V_{\rm int}(\{\phi_i\},\{\phi_j\}) =\frac{1}{2} \sum_{i\neq j}\sum_jV_{ij}(\phi_i,\phi_j), \label{n2}\end{equation} with $V_{ij (\phi_i,\phi_j)$ being the interaction potential between the fields $\phi_i$ and $\phi_j$.}

elements.section-2411-006: Field-theoretic inconsistency of the fluid model

elements.sent-2411-185: From Eq.~(\ref{vintp ), we have $V'_{\rm int (\chi,\phi)=0$, i.e., $V_{\rm int (\chi,\phi)$ is independent of time and so we write \begin{equation}V_{\rm int}(\chi,\phi)=\frac{\lambda}{2} \chi^2 \phi^2 = c. \label{cte}\end{equation} Here $c$ is a constant which gives $\chi=\pm \sqrt{\frac{2c {\lambda \phi$, i.e., the fields $\chi$ and $\phi$ are not independent but one is determined in terms of the other which is obviously false.}}}}}}
elements.section-2411-007: Dark matter and dark energy background equations
elements.sent-2411-186: In this section, we derive the background equations that describe the time evolution of the DM and DE fields in a flat FLRW metric.
elements.sent-2411-187: In our interacting quintessence-dark matter model (QCDM), $\chi$ represents a dark matter field while $\phi$ is a dark energy field and are governed by the potentials \begin{equation}\label{v1} V_1(\chi)=\frac{1}{2}m_\chi^2\chi^2, \label{v2} V_2(\phi)=\mu^4\left[1+\cos\left(\frac{\phi}{F}\right)\right].\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-188: The interaction potential between $\chi$ and $\phi$ is based on field theory and is given by \begin{equation}V_{\rm int}(\phi,\chi)\equiv V_{12}(\phi,\chi)=\frac{\lambda}{2}\chi^2\phi^2. \label{v3}\end{equation} We track the evolution of the two background fields $\chi$ and $\phi$ by solving the Klein-Gordon (KG) equations \begin{equation}\label{kgc0} \chi_0^{\prime\prime}+2H\chi_0^\prime+a^2(V_{1,\chi}+V_{12,\chi})=0, \phi_0^{\prime\prime}+2H\phi_0^\prime+a^2(V_{2,\phi}+V_{12,\phi})=0, \label{kgp0}\end{equation} where the `0' subscript denotes a background field.
elements.sent-2411-189: For a DM potential of the form given by Eq.~(\ref{v1 ), the field undergoes rapid oscillations when $\mathcal{H /m_\chi\ll 1$~\cite{Turner:1983he,Urena-Lopez:2015gur , making a numerical solution intractable.}}}
elements.sent-2411-190: In order to efficiently solve the background evolution equation for the DM field, we recast Eq.~(\ref{kgc0 ) in a different form.}
elements.sent-2411-191: First, let us define the DM energy density as $\tilde{\rho _\chi=\rho_\chi-V_{12}$ so that the modified energy density fraction becomes \begin{equation}\tilde{\Omega}_\chi\equiv\frac{\tilde{\rho}_\chi}{\rho_{\rm cr}} =\frac{\kappa^2}{6H^2}\Big(\chi^{\prime 2}_0+2a^2V_1(\chi_0)\Big), \label{om-tilde}\end{equation} where $\kappa\equiv\sqrt{8\pi G}$.}
elements.sent-2411-192: Based on Eq.~(\ref{om-tilde ), we define the following new dimensionless variables\cite{Copeland:1997et,Garcia-Arroyo:2024tqq \begin{equation}\label{var-tilde-1} \tilde{\Omega}_\chi^{1/2}\sin\left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)=\frac{\kappa \chi^\prime}{\sqrt{6}H}, \label{var-tilde-2} \tilde{\Omega}_\chi^{1/2}\cos\left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)=\frac{\kappa a V^{1/2}_{1}}{\sqrt{3}H}, y=-\frac{2\sqrt{2}\,a}{H}\partial_\chi V^{1/2}_{1}. \label{var-tilde-3}\end{equation} The advantage gained by introducing the new variables is that the rapid oscillations of the DM field can be absorbed into the $\theta$ variable.}}
elements.sent-2411-193: Using the new variables defined by Eqs.~(\ref{var-tilde-1 )$-$(\ref{var-tilde-3 ), we recast the KG equation of the field $\chi$ into a set of three coupled first order differential equations in $\Omega_\chi$, $\theta$ and $y$.}}
elements.sent-2411-194: After some lengthy, but otherwise straightforward, calculation, we arrive at \begin{equation}\Omega^\prime_\chi =3H\Omega_\chi(w_T-w_\chi) +\frac{\kappa^2 a^2}{3H^2}\Bigg[H(1+3w_\chi)V_{12}-\chi^\prime V_{12,\chi}\Bigg], \label{omc0} \theta^\prime =-3H\sin\theta+Hy -\frac{\kappa^2 a^2}{3H^2\tilde{\Omega}_\chi}\Bigg(2H V_{12}+\chi^\prime V_{12,\chi}\Bigg)\cot\frac{\theta}{2}, \label{thetac0} y^\prime =\frac{3}{2}{\cal H}(1+w_T)y\,, \label{yeq0}\end{equation} with the total EoS $w_T=\sum p_i/\sum\rho_i$, where the sum is over all species (baryons, photons, neutrinos, DM and DE) and $\tilde{\Omega _\chi=\Omega_\chi-(\kappa^2 a^2/3\mathcal{H ^2)V_{12}$.}}

elements.sent-2411-281: In this work we discussed a field-theoretic model of cosmology with an interaction term between dark matter and dark energy which are described by ultralight spin zero fields.
elements.sent-2411-282: We show that the fluid formalism used to describe dark matter-dark energy interaction is inconsistent in a field-theory approach.
elements.sent-2411-283: Furthermore, considering a specific dark energy potential and a dark matter-dark energy interaction term, we study the cosmological implication of such a model on the evolution of the dark energy equation of state.
elements.sent-2411-284: We distinguish between two regimes based on the DM-DE interaction strength: the strong coupling regime with $\lambda\geq 10^{-2}$ and the weak coupling region with $\lambda<10^{-2}$.
elements.sent-2411-285: The strong coupling regime induces a transmutation of the dark energy equation of state which is visible in its evolution from a thawing behavior to a scaling freezing behavior.
elements.sent-2411-286: A dark energy equation of state from a freezing quintessence at current times is at odds with the DESI results and so the strong coupling regime can be ruled out.
elements.sent-2411-287: The weak coupling regime, on the other hand, is shown to produce results consistent with DESI, aligned with the prediction of an evolving equation of state.
elements.sent-2411-288: We provide a fit to the dark energy equation of state, which, to leading order in $(1-a)$, can produce $w_0$ and $w_a$ values consistent with DESI without crossing the phantom divide.
elements.sent-2411-289: Using the three-data set combinations from DESI, we derive an upper limit on the value of $\lambda$ and show that many benchmark points close to this upper limit remain consistent with DESI.

elements.sent-2411-362: In our analysis, we use the following data sets:.
elements.sent-2411-363: The Planck 2018 temperature anisotropies and polarization measurements.
elements.sent-2411-364: The temperature and polarization (TT TE EE) likelihoods include low multipole data ($\ell<30$)~\cite{Planck:2018vyg,Planck:2018nkj,Planck:2019nip .}
elements.sent-2411-365: The high multipole likelihood includes: $30\lesssim \ell\lesssim 2500$ for the TT spectrum and $30\lesssim \ell\lesssim 2000$ for the TE and EE spectra.
elements.sent-2411-366: The low-E polarization likelihood includes $2\leq \ell\leq 30$ for the EE spectrum.
elements.sent-2411-367: We also include the Planck 2018 lensing likelihood~\cite{Planck:2018lbu which is inferred from the lensing potential power spectrum.}
elements.sent-2411-368: This whole data set is referred to as CMB.
elements.sent-2411-369: Baryon Acoustic Oscillations (from DESI-DR2): We include BAO measurements from DESI's second data release, which takes into account observations of galaxies and quasars~\cite{DESI:2025zgx , as well as Lyman-$\alpha$ tracers~\cite{DESI:2025zpo .}}
elements.sent-2411-370: These measurements cover both isotropic and anisotropic BAO constraints over $0.295\leq z\leq 2.330$, divided into nine redshift bins.
elements.sent-2411-371: This data set is referred to as DESI.
elements.sent-2411-372: The combination PantheonPlus+SH0ES~\cite{Brout:2022vxf,Riess:2021jrx data set which uses an additional Cepheid distance as a calibrator of the Supernova SNIa intrinsic magnitude.}
elements.sent-2411-373: This data set is referred to as PPS.
elements.sent-2411-374: Union 3.0: The Union 3.0 database, consisting of 2087 SN Ia within the range $0.001<z<2.260$~\cite{Rubin:2023jdq .}
elements.sent-2411-375: The data set has 1363 SN Ia in common with the PantheonPlus sample and uses a special and different treatment of systematic errors and uncertainties by employing Bayesian hierarchical modeling.
elements.sent-2411-376: We refer to this dataset as Union3.
elements.sent-2411-377: DESY5: In their Year 5 data release, the Dark Energy Survey (DES) presented results based on a newly published, uniformly selected sample of 1635 photometrically classified Type Ia supernovae, covering redshifts from $0.1 < z < 1.3$~\cite{DES:2024jxu .}
elements.sent-2411-378: This sample is supplemented by 194 low-redshift SN Ia, drawn from the PantheonPlus dataset, within the range $0.025 < z < 0.1$.
elements.sent-2411-379: We refer to this combined dataset as DESY5.
elements.sent-2411-380: The Boltzmann solver \code{CLASS is interfaced with \code{Cobaya ~\cite{Torrado:2020dgo , a code for sampling and statistical modeling, to perform a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) analysis of the model parameter space which allows us to extract constraints on the cosmological parameters through Bayesian inference.}}}
elements.sent-2411-381: We monitor the convergence of the chains using the Gelman-Rubin~\cite{Gelman:1992zz criterion $R-1<0.05$.}
elements.sent-2411-382: After convergence, the chains are analyzed with \code{GetDist ~\cite{Lewis:2019xzd}\footnote{\url{https://github.com/cmbant/getdist , a package allowing for the extraction of numerical results, including 1D posteriors and 2D marginalized probability contours.}}}
elements.sent-2411-383: The sampling parameters consist of the baseline $\Lambda$CDM parameters along with the four additional free parameters of our model \begin{equation}\underbrace{\Omega_{\rm b} h^2, ~~\Omega_\chi, ~~z_{\rm reio}, ~~100\theta_s, ~~\log(10^{10}A_s), ~~n_s}_{\Lambda\text{CDM}}, ~~\log\mu^4,~~F,~~\phi_{\rm ini},~~\log\lambda\,,\end{equation} where $\Omega_\chi$ ($\Omega_{\rm b}$) is the DM (baryon) fractional density, $A_s$ is the amplitude of primordial fluctuations, $n_s$ is the spectral index of the primordial power spectrum, $\theta_s$ is the angular scale of the sound horizon at the surface of last scattering, and $z_{\rm reio}$ is the redshift at reionization.

elements.section-2411-016: On consistent fluid equations for interacting fields
elements.sent-2411-433: In this appendix we give further details of the fluid equations arising from a Lagrangian approach which generates sources to the fluid equations in a consistent manner once the interaction between the fields is specified in the Lagrangian.
elements.sent-2411-434: For simplicity we will work with cosmic time and then convert the final result for conformal time.
elements.sent-2411-435: For two interacting scalar fields $\phi$ and $\chi$ the total Lagrangian is given by \begin{equation}\mathcal{L} = -\frac{1}{2} \partial_\mu \phi \partial^\mu \phi - V_1(\phi) - \frac{1}{2} \partial_\mu \chi \partial^\mu \chi - V_2(\chi) - V_{12}(\phi, \chi),\end{equation} where $V_{12 (\phi, \chi)$ specifies the interaction between the fields $\phi$ and $\chi$.}

elements.formula-2411-072: \begin{equation}\rho_\phi = \frac{1}{2} \dot{\phi}^2 + V_1(\phi) + V_{12}(\phi, \chi), p_\phi = \frac{1}{2} \dot{\phi}^2 - V_1(\phi) - V_{12}(\phi, \chi), \rho_\chi = \frac{1}{2} \dot{\chi}^2 + V_2(\chi) + V_{12}(\phi, \chi), p_\chi = \frac{1}{2} \dot{\chi}^2 - V_2(\chi) - V_{12}(\phi, \chi).\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-439: The above lead to the total energy density and pressure so that \begin{equation}\rho\equiv \rho_{total} = \frac{1}{2} (\dot{\phi}^2 + \dot{\chi}^2) + V_1 + V_2 + V_{12}, p\equiv p_{total} = \frac{1}{2} (\dot{\phi}^2 + \dot{\chi}^2) - V_1 - V_2 - V_{12}.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-073: \begin{equation}\rho\equiv \rho_{total} = \frac{1}{2} (\dot{\phi}^2 + \dot{\chi}^2) + V_1 + V_2 + V_{12}, p\equiv p_{total} = \frac{1}{2} (\dot{\phi}^2 + \dot{\chi}^2) - V_1 - V_2 - V_{12}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-440: One may note the following relation between $\rho$ and ($\rho_\phi+ \rho_\chi$) and a similar relation between $p$ and ($p_\phi+p_\chi$). \begin{equation}\label{appen1} \rho = \rho_\phi+ \rho_\chi -V_{12}, p = p_\phi+ p_\chi +V_{12}. \label{appen2}\end{equation} Next we compute the fluid equations for the fields $\phi$ and $\chi$.
elements.sent-2411-441: Here we begin with the Klein-Gordon equations in an expanding universe assuming spatial homogeneity, so that the equations $\phi$ and $\chi$ are \begin{equation}\ddot{\phi} + 3H\dot{\phi} + \frac{\partial V_1}{\partial \phi} + \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \phi} = 0, \ddot{\chi} + 3H\dot{\chi} + \frac{\partial V_2}{\partial \chi} + \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \chi} = 0.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-442: Using the equations of motion the corresponding individual continuity equations for the densities for $\phi$ and $\chi$ directly follow and are given by \begin{equation}\label{appen3} \dot{\rho}_\phi + 3H(\rho_\phi + p_\phi) = \dot{\chi} \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \chi} \equiv Q_\phi\,, \dot{\rho}_\chi + 3H(\rho_\chi + p_\chi) = \dot{\phi} \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \phi} \equiv Q_\chi\,. \label{appen4}\end{equation} Note that \begin{equation}Q_\phi+Q_\chi = \dot{\chi} \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \chi} + \dot{\phi} \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \phi}=\dot {V}_{12}\,. \label{appen5}\end{equation} Using Eqs.~(\ref{appen1 ),~(\ref{appen2 ) and Eqs.~(\ref{appen3 )$-$(\ref{appen5 ) one finds that the total density $\rho$ satisfies the relation \begin{equation}\dot{\rho} + 3H(\rho + p) = 0. \label{appen6}\end{equation} Thus the total energy is automatically conserved with $Q_\phi$ and $Q_\chi$ as determined by the Lagrangian equations without making an additional assumption.}}}}
elements.formula-2411-076: \begin{equation}\label{appen3} \dot{\rho}_\phi + 3H(\rho_\phi + p_\phi) = \dot{\chi} \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \chi} \equiv Q_\phi\,, \dot{\rho}_\chi + 3H(\rho_\chi + p_\chi) = \dot{\phi} \frac{\partial V_{12}}{\partial \phi} \equiv Q_\chi\,. \label{appen4}\end{equation}
